clusters.label: Forest Landscape Restoration Assessment and Governance
clusters.description: This cluster represents practical methodologies and analytical frameworks for assessing restoration opportunities, monitoring implementation, and mapping the social networks and actors that drive landscape restoration outcomes.
key_items: Collect Earth mapathons for monitoring forest and landscape restoration
ROAM (Restoration Opportunities Assessment Methodology)
InVEST ecosystem service model
Co$ting Nature ecosystem service model
WaterWorld ecosystem service model
ARIES ecosystem service model
LPJ-GUESS ecosystem service model
The Restoration Diagnostic three-step assessment methodology
TerraMatch / Restore Local framework for monitoring, reporting, and verification
Sustainability Index for Landscape Restoration (SILR)
Global Landscapes Forum social media analysis across Nairobi and Bonn conferences
Malawi Integrated Household Panel Survey with Difference-in-Difference causal analysis of farmland restoration
Rwanda restoration governance network mapping across information, finance, and seedling flows
Transfer learning CNN approach to classify natural, monoculture, and agroforestry systems in Ghana

clusters.label: Farmer-Led Restoration Finance and Scale
clusters.description: This cluster represents knowledge about financing mechanisms, business models, and policy instruments that enable smallholder farmers and restoration entrepreneurs across Africa and Latin America to restore degraded landscapes while generating livelihoods and achieving conservation outcomes at scale.
key_items: KOFAR restoration business (Kenya)
Lentera Africa restoration business (Kenya)
TerraFund for AFR100 cohort investment mechanism ($15M across 27 countries)
Patricie Nyirabahinzi's Vernonia amygdalina (bitterleaf) cultivation (Rwanda, Gatsibo District)
Paran Women Group collective financing model (Kenya, 64 Indigenous women's groups)
Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) in the Sahel
Ali Malam and Sakina Mati's radio-based farmer peer-to-peer diffusion (Niger)
Guatemala's PINFOR forestry incentive program (1996–2016)
Guatemala's PINPEP smallholder forestry program (since 2010)
Guatemala's PROBOSQUE forestry program (2046)
Costa Rica's PPSA Payment for Ecosystem Services program (established 1996)
Mexico's CONAFOR national PES system (since 2003)
Marliane das Chagas Soares's agroforestry transition in Juruti, Pará (Brazil)

clusters.label: Economic Viability of Landscape Restoration
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence on the financial returns, cost-benefit analyses, and economic incentives for scaling tree-based restoration, agroforestry, and tenure security across tropical regions, demonstrating that conservation and restoration investments are economically viable while addressing tenure barriers and research gaps.
key_items: MapTenure web-based platform for resolving tenure ambiguity in Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh
Trees Outside Forests (ToF) policy incentives across Gujarat, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Odisha, Punjab, and Telangana
Community forest tenure security benefit-cost analysis in Brazil and Guatemala
VERENA Investment Tool for native tree species and agroforestry systems in Brazil
Collect Earth mapathons combined with field assessments in Sidhi district, Madhya Pradesh
Enrichment planting of naturally regenerating forests with native non-timber forest product species across 14 systems in Latin America, Africa, and Asia
Matching analysis methodology applied to Indigenous land rights in Bolivia, Brazil, and Colombia to quantify avoided deforestation and carbon benefits
Native-species reforestation economic models in Brazil including silviculture, agroforestry systems, and crop-livestock-forest integration with documented ROI rates
Assisted Natural Regeneration (ANR) field practices for accelerating forest and landscape restoration
CHARM model projections for analyzing competing land demands between food, carbon, biodiversity, and construction wood
Payments for ecosystem services mechanisms for Indigenous peoples and local communities in Guatemala through INAB, Utz Che', and FEDECOVERA programs
Six economic and political economy barriers framework with scaling strategies for forest conservation and restoration
ANR Alliance's comprehensive synthesis of Assisted Natural Regeneration policies, costs, and enabling factors

clusters.label: Monitoring and Measuring Tree Restoration at Scale
clusters.description: This cluster addresses the technical, methodological, and practical challenges of tracking, verifying, and implementing large-scale tree restoration initiatives through satellite monitoring, ground-truthing, and stakeholder engagement while ensuring ecological and social integrity.
key_items: Trillion Trees initiative
TerraMatch platform
Initiative 20x20
Global Forest Watch
Collect Earth
Priceless Planet Coalition
1t.org
Turkey 11 million saplings failure
Kenya mesquite introduction
University of Maryland satellite data on tree cover (Latin America 2015–2023)
Mekong region forest monitoring
Tijuca urban forest restoration project
Atlantic Forest restoration in Salvador and São Paulo
REDD+ in the Paris Agreement Article 5

clusters.label: Climate-Resilient Agriculture and Nature-Based Solutions
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence and actionable guidance on transformative agricultural adaptation and nature-based solutions for building food security and climate resilience, targeting policymakers, funders, researchers, and financial institutions across Sub-Saharan Africa and globally.
key_items: Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) in Malawi
Integrated crop-livestock-forestry systems
Agroforestry practices
Direct seeding technique
Rainwater harvesting methods
Tree-based agricultural systems in Malawi
The INSPIRE framework for nature-based solutions investment
Glasgow Declaration on climate adaptation
Adaptation Fund pledges for nature-based solutions
Gender-responsive agricultural adaptation funding mechanisms
WRI Brasil / GIZ working paper on sustainable agricultural practices
World Bank PROFOR program for tree-based systems
MDB project data analysis on nature-based solutions financing

clusters.label: Scaling Restoration Through Policy, Technology, and Business
clusters.description: This cluster represents knowledge on how landscape restoration can be rapidly scaled to meet global commitments (350 Mha by 2030) through aligned policy frameworks, technological innovation for site selection, and economic models that create jobs and revenue while addressing enforcement barriers and land tenure challenges.
key_items: NY Declaration of Trees, Lands and Forests (350 Mha target by 2030)
Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) in Niger, Ethiopia, and Costa Rica
DINOv3-based remote sensing AI model for tree detection and restoration finance allocation
AFR100 (African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative) with 21 participating nations
Bonn Challenge restoration commitments alignment with Paris Agreement NDCs
EcoPlanet Bamboo restoration enterprise
BioCarbon Engineering drone tree-planting technology
Tentree apparel-linked tree-planting model
Federal US tree restoration investment program ($4–4.5 billion annually for 150,000+ jobs)
Landscapes of Opportunity 2011 global assessment framework
Multi-stakeholder platforms (MSPs) for landscape restoration in Rwanda and Malawi
Roots of Prosperity report benefit-cost analysis ($7–30 per $1 invested)
Guatemala landholder forest tenure and enforcement case study
Ghana mangrove restoration financing case

clusters.label: Financing and Scaling Landscape Restoration
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence on the economic case for restoration, practical implementation pathways, and mechanisms—from public incentives to private investment—for financing and scaling land and forest restoration at regional and national levels.
key_items: Initiative 20x20 target for restoring 20 million hectares in Latin America and the Caribbean
Land Life Company Series A fundraising
Sahel regreening evidence base
Agricultural subsidy repurposing programs in India, Brazil, and Mexico
Payment for Ecosystem Services (PES)
Blended finance
Water funds
Green bonds
Agroforestry
Conservation agriculture
Rainwater harvesting
Integrated soil fertility management
Public economic incentive programs across six Latin American countries
Nature-based watershed management in Ethiopia
